---
title: "Lab notebook"
subtitle: "JP Flores · Phanstiel → Scavuzzo → Gabriele labs"
date: today
---

This is a living record of wet lab experiments, protocols, and reading notes.
It is not a computational notebook — that lives separately.
Every experiment entry is one `.qmd` file per run, committed to git when complete.

## Quick links

| Section | What's in it |
|---------|-------------|
| [Protocols](protocols/index.qmd) | Shiny QMDs — configure inputs, print to PDF, take to bench |
| [Experiments](experiments/index.qmd) | One entry per experiment run, filed by date |
| [Reading notes](reading-notes/index.qmd) | Protocol-adjacent papers worth annotating in depth |
| [Aims](aims/index.qmd) | F32, HFSP, Fulbright — living documents |

## How to use this notebook

**Starting a new experiment:**

```bash
# Copy the relevant template
cp experiments/_templates/HiC_entry_template.qmd \
   experiments/$(date +%Y-%m-%d)_HiC-[target]-[celltype].qmd

# Open in Positron and fill in the planning section
# Then each evening: fill in that day's section, save

# When the experiment is fully done:
git add experiments/YYYY-MM-DD_HiC-target-celltype.qmd
git commit -m "Hi-C [target] [celltype] — [one-line result summary]"
```

**Printing a protocol:**

```bash
quarto serve protocols/HiC_protocol.qmd
# Configure Shiny inputs in browser → Cmd+P → Save as PDF
```

**Rebuilding the site:**

```bash
quarto render
# Open _site/index.html in browser
```

## Recent experiments

```{r}
#| output: asis
library(fs)
library(tidyverse)

entries <- dir_ls("experiments", glob = "*.qmd", recurse = FALSE) |>
  path_file() |>
  discard(~ str_starts(., "_") | str_starts(., "index"))

if (length(entries) == 0) {
  cat("_No experiment entries yet. Run your first experiment!_\n")
} else {
  entries |>
    sort(decreasing = TRUE) |>
    head(10) |>
    walk(~ cat(paste0("- [", path_ext_remove(.), "](experiments/", ., ")\n")))
}
```
